mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, invalidating the labor shock penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series are in their top quartile, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle recovery phases, with a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases show zero predictive power for 5-day SPX direction, with a hit rate indistinguishable from random chance (50%) over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 2.5% annual threshold, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth surpasses 2.5%, invalidating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption channels, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal produces a negative or flat 20-day forward return distribution, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.1 in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, with a positive Rank IC exceeding 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
